fact: Meus Remedios is a medication management PWA in Portuguese Brazilian using React 19, Vite 7, Supabase, Zod, and Framer Motion 12 (v3.0.0)
Code is written in English, but UI, docs, and error messages are in Portuguese Brazilian
API services are located in src/services/api/, only 2 services without feature equivalent
Feature services are located at src/features/*/services/*.js (e.g., @medications/services/medicineService)
Shared services are in src/shared/services/*.js, including cachedServices and migrationService
Log service canonical location is src/shared/services/api/logService.js
All Zod schemas have a single canonical location at src/schemas/*.js
Utility functions are located in src/utils/*.js (adherenceLogic, dateUtils, titrationUtils, etc.)
Shared hooks are in src/shared/hooks/*.js (useCachedQuery, useTheme, useHapticFeedback, useShake)
Shared components are in src/shared/components/**/*.jsx, organized by domain (ui/, gamification/, log/, onboarding/, pwa/)
Feature-specific components are in src/features/*/components/*.jsx
Supabase client is imported from @shared/utils/supabase (was previously at src/lib/supabase.js, now deleted)
Cache utility is imported from @shared/utils/queryCache (was previously at src/lib/queryCache.js, now deleted)
Wave 9 removed src/lib/, src/hooks/, src/components/, src/shared/constants/, and src/features/*/constants/ directories
ESLint no-restricted-imports rule blocks imports from legacy paths to enforce canonical locations
Path alias @ resolves to src/

Vite path aliases: @features→src/features, @shared→src/shared, @services→src/services, @dashboard→src/features/dashboard, @medications→src/features/medications, @protocols→src/features/protocols, @stock→src/features/stock, @adherence→src/features/adherence, @schemas→src/schemas, @utils→src/utils
MedicineForm component accepts props: onSuccess, autoAdvance=false, showCancelButton=true
ProtocolForm component accepts props: mode='full'|'simple', preselectedMedicine
Calendar component accepts props: enableLazyLoad, enableSwipe, enableMonthPicker
AlertList component accepts variant prop: 'smart'|'stock'
LogForm component always requires treatmentPlans prop for bulk registration
Frequency constants: ['diario', 'dias_alternados', 'semanal', 'personalizado', 'quando_necessario']
Medicine type constants: ['comprimido', 'capsula', 'liquido', 'injecao', 'pomada', 'spray', 'outro']
Weekday constants in Portuguese: ['domingo', 'segunda', 'terca', 'quarta', 'quinta', 'sexta', 'sabado']
Glassmorphism tokens: --glass-light (rgba 0.03), --glass-standard (0.08), --glass-heavy (0.15), --glass-hero (0.2)
Modal max-height must never be 100vh; use 85vh to account for BottomNav component
Modal padding-bottom should be at least 60px to provide scroll space
JSX arrows must use {'<'} and {'>'} syntax to avoid parsing errors
correct: Use {'<'} instead of < and {'>'} instead of > inside JSX

fact: Stock CRITICAL level: threshold < 7 days, color #ef4444, label 'Critico'
Stock LOW level: threshold < 14 days, color #f59e0b, label 'Baixo'
Stock NORMAL level: threshold < 30 days, color #22c55e, label 'Normal'
Stock HIGH level: threshold >= 30 days, color #3b82f6, label 'Bom'
Dosage calculation: dosage_real = dosage_per_intake (pills) × dosage_per_pill (mg), quantity_taken always in pills not mg
SWR cache pattern: import cachedMedicineService from @services/api/cachedServices, cache invalidates automatically after mutations, manual invalidation via queryCache.delete()
All user data tables MUST have RLS enabled to enforce access control
Frontend uses Supabase anon key (public), RLS enforces per-user access via auth.uid()
Backend (server/) uses Supabase service_role (privileged) key only
Standard RLS policy: USING (user_id = auth.uid())
Vercel Hobby Plan: max 12 serverless functions per deployment
Files in _-prefixed directories (e.g., _handlers/) are NOT counted as serverless functions
Max serverless function duration: 300 seconds (5 min) on Fluid Compute
Max request body size: 4.5 MB
Function invocations quota: 1,000,000 per month on free tier
Current Vercel function budget: 6/12 used (DLQ router, Gemini reviews, health notifications, notify cron, share, telegram)
Router pattern: dispatch by action (query param or URL segment), handlers extracted to _handlers/ (not counted as functions)
Supabase Auth JWT pattern: supabase.auth.getUser(token) + admin check, used by DLQ router and share.js
GitHub Actions JWT pattern: jwt.verify(token, VERCEL_GITHUB_ACTIONS_SECRET), used by Gemini persist/create-issues/update-status
Webhook secret pattern: crypto.timingSafeEqual vs AGENT_WEBHOOK_SECRET, used by Gemini batch-update
Cron secret pattern: direct comparison vs CRON_SECRET, used by notify.js
Use conditional dotenv: if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production') — Vercel injects env vars automatically in production
correct: Never force dotenv in production; check NODE_ENV first

fact: Router rewrite pattern: { 'source': '/api/dlq/:id/retry', 'destination': '/api/dlq.js?action=retry&id=:id' }
Catch-all rewrite /(.*) → /index.html MUST be the LAST rewrite in vercel.json
correct: Never place catch-all rewrite before specific API routes

fact: MarkdownV2 escaping priority: backslash FIRST, then underscores, asterisks, brackets, parens, tildes, backticks, angle brackets, hashes, plus, minus, equals, pipes, braces, dots, exclamation
correct: Always escape backslash first in MarkdownV2; wrong order breaks Telegram messages

fact: ESLint varsIgnorePattern for Framer Motion: '^(motion|AnimatePresence|[A-Z_])'
Every delegation to Code mode MUST include: read .agent/memory/rules.json, read .agent/memory/anti-patterns.json, check for duplicate files
Local development: use npm run test:fast (1 thread, ~6.5 min), safest baseline for daily development before commit
Fast iteration mode: npx vitest run --singleThread=false --maxThreads=2 (~3-4 min) but risky for race conditions
Low-RAM machines (8GB): npm run test:lowram uses sequential execution (~20 min), no race condition risk
Agent validation: npm run validate:agent (4+ threads, <10 min) with kill-switch enforced
CI/CD (GitHub Actions): npm run validate:full (4+ threads, <5 min) with full coverage and unlimited resources
Test files must be <300 lines each, one logical unit per file (one hook, component, or service), split by feature/scope not test type
Test cache cleanup: cancelGarbageCollection() in beforeAll, clearCache() + vi.clearAllMocks() + vi.clearAllTimers() in afterEach, restartGarbageCollection() in afterAll
Individual test timeout: testTimeout: 10000 (10 sec) to fail fast on slow tests
Hook timeout: hookTimeout: 10000 (10 sec)
Teardown timeout: teardownTimeout: 5000 (5 sec)
Agent kill-switch: validate:agent enforces 10-min global timeout (exit code 124)
Safe for parallel testing: pure unit tests with mocked dependencies and no shared state
Risky for parallel testing: tests sharing localStorage, global cache, or fake timers
correct: Never parallelize tests with shared state; use 1 thread baseline

fact: Very risky for parallel testing: tests modifying Supabase mocks or shared DOM
correct: Always use 1 thread for integration tests touching mocked Supabase or DOM

fact: Extraction inventory maps ~100 files by category (PURE, ADAPTER_REQUIRED, PLATFORM_WEB, SHARED_TOKEN, DO_NOT_SHARE). Guides code organization for Phases 1-3.
Shared boundary matrix defines package boundaries: packages/core (Fase 2), packages/design-tokens (Fase 2), packages/storage/config/shared-data (Fase 3). Web stays at root Phases 0-6 (ADR-026).
